draft_v1: {"schema_v":1,"recipe":{"title":"DRIED MIRABELLE PLUM SKINS, A CANDY MADE FROM ROSEMARY","description":"I n the late summer, wild plums ripen all over the island during a very short season. These plums can be red or yellow, and they have larger pits and thicker skin than cultivated plums. We collected them and experimented, working our way toward a plum dessert. One trial had the plums stewing uncovered in a pot, and we found that the skins floating to the top were exposed to the air and became leathery, with a concentrated flavor. After drying those skins a touch more in front of the hearth, they were perfect.","yield_phrase":"4"},"steps":[{"instruction":"Quarter 3 of the best-looking plums and discard the pits. Carefully slice the skins from each quarter, leaving them with about 1/8 inch/3 mm of flesh. Cover and refrigerate the skins and place the scrap in a medium metal mixing bowl.","ingredient_lines":[{"raw_ingredient_text":"16 Mirabelle plums (about 1 kg)"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"Stock syrup (page 256)"},{"raw_ingredient_text":"1 (10-inch/25 cm) square rosemary candy (page 257), broken into 1/2-inch/1 cm shards"}]}]}
